---
title: "Pandas"
date: 2026-02-15
categories: [tool, python, data-analysis]
status: "Active Use"
link: "https://pandas.pydata.org"
description: "Essential Python library for data manipulation and analysis"
---

## What It Does

Pandas is the foundational data analysis library for Python. It provides DataFrames (think Excel spreadsheets in code) and powerful tools for cleaning, transforming, and analyzing data.

## Why I Bookmarked It

Every single data analysis project I do starts with Pandas. It's the Swiss Army knife of data manipulation. Whether I'm cleaning API responses or calculating sports statistics, Pandas makes it simple.

## Hello World Example

```python
import pandas as pd

# Create a DataFrame from dict
data = {
    'team': ['Oklahoma', 'Texas', 'Alabama'],
    'wins': [10, 12, 11],
    'losses': [3, 2, 2]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Calculate win percentage
df['win_pct'] = df['wins'] / (df['wins'] + df['losses'])

print(df)
```

## Verdict

**Status: Active Use** - Non-negotiable foundation of my data stack.

- **Pros:** Industry standard, massive community, handles any data format
- **Cons:** Memory-intensive for huge datasets (use Polars for that)
- **Use Cases:** Every data analysis project, API response parsing, CSV manipulation

## Resources

- [Official Docs](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/)
- [10 Minutes to Pandas](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/user_guide/10min.html)
- [My Pandas Tutorials](../code/index.html)
